authoritative_sources.title: Health Clubs Consumer Brief
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state consumer-regulator guidance for health-club membership registration, contract length, cancellation rights, online termination expectations, and refund or closure handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Membership-contract review, cancellation-path handling, and closure or refund boundary control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains membership-status, cancellation, freeze, and facility-closure scenarios so the lane asks for agreement version, jurisdiction, enrollment channel, and closure or refund evidence before resolving or escalating.

authoritative_sources.title: Ed Tech Provider Chegg to Pay $7.5 Million to Settle FTC Allegations Concerning Unlawful Cancellation Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC enforcement guidance summarizing the current ROSCA baseline requiring clear material terms, express informed consent, and simple ways to stop recurring charges.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Recurring-charge handling, online cancellation, and unsupported cancellation-path escalation
used_for: consumer-protection failure modes for online memberships
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from inventing restrictive cancellation paths or burying recurring-charge terms when a membership was purchased or renewed online.

authoritative_sources.title: Health & Fitness Association Club Membership Eligibility Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HFA is the recognized public trade association for health and fitness clubs, and its membership standards define complaint handling, pre-sell fee, pre-activity screening, emergency planning, and professional-competence expectations that directly shape membership workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint handling, member-readiness checks, and fitness-club safety boundary awareness
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from treating fitness or wellness memberships as ordinary recurring billing when screening, emergency response, or qualified-program-supervision expectations materially change the workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: Spa Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISPA is the recognized public trade association for spa operators, and its manual provides vendor-neutral operational guidance for front-desk coverage, guest history records, booking ownership, service-ticket changes, and receptionist handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Front-desk intake, guest-history record review, booking ownership, and service-ticket traceability
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Shapes the artifact list around guest history records, booking logs, service-ticket updates, and receptionist ownership so spa or wellness member requests stay tied to operational records rather than informal notes.

authoritative_sources.title: Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISPA's public Code of Conduct is a recognized communication and guest-expectation framework used across spa environments for orientation, front-desk posting, and respectful member-facing language.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer communication, expectation setting, and complaint-safe messaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains member-facing communication so the lane uses clear, respectful, expectation-setting language instead of improvised or sales-heavy responses during disputes or handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: Can card verification codes be stored for card-on-file or recurring transactions?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card controls. This official FAQ directly answers card-on-file and recurring-transaction handling for memberships.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Recurring-payment setup, card-on-file handling, and payment-control refusal posture
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from storing or requesting prohibited verification-code data as a workaround for recurring wellness memberships and keeps card-on-file handling inside approved payment flows.

authoritative_sources.title: What is the maximum period of time that cardholder data can be stored?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC's official FAQ is authoritative for cardholder-data retention, disposal, and protection requirements relevant to recurring-membership billing setups.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Stored-payment evidence handling and retention-aware escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from treating locally stored payment details as normal membership notes and forces secure deletion or processor-based retrieval when card data is no longer required.

authoritative_sources.title: Esthetics
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Board of Barbering and Cosmetology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state licensing-board publication defining esthetics scope and prohibited activities in beauty and personal-care settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Licensed-service boundary checks and esthetics-scope escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from answering practitioner-scope or treatment-appropriateness questions as if they were routine membership administration.

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Spa Services Advisory
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public-health advisory explaining when spa or wellness establishments become regulated medical-service environments subject to clinic or practitioner oversight.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Medical-spa boundary recognition, clinic-licensure awareness, and clinical-owner escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds a concrete refusal trigger for med-spa membership questions that cross from administrative membership work into medical-service or clinic-regulated territory.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and member-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm member, location, jurisdiction, membership tier, agreement version, enrollment channel, and requested outcome before acting.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Agreement, consent, and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Review signed agreement, auto-renew or EFT authorization, waiver or guest-history record, pre-activity screening completion, and policy version before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Billing, credit, and booking state retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Compare CRM, POS or subscription, payment-processor, booking, attendance, and service-credit records to establish documented workflow state.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and bounded communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using documented membership status, cancellation or freeze rules, booking constraints, and guest-safe communication without promising exceptions or clinical outcomes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Clinical, licensed-service, repeat-failure, and policy-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unsupported exceptions, repeat failures, med-spa or esthetics boundaries, payment-control issues, and public-source or tenant-policy conflicts for the correct next owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: membership agreements, agreement-version history, and auto-renew or EFT authorization records
CRM member profiles, POS or subscription ledgers, payment-processor status records, and failed-payment or chargeback notes
booking calendars, attendance logs, service tickets, waitlists, no-show records, and service-credit or package ledgers
guest history records, waivers, pre-activity screening completion, contraindication or restriction notes, and provider-assignment details
refund requests, freeze or cancellation forms, manager approvals, communication logs, and complaint or incident records
provider roster, licensure or clinic-boundary references, and escalation packets for med-spa, licensed-service, or financial exceptions

allowed: Classify wellness-membership work as in-scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented agreement, payment, booking, waiver, and complaint artifacts.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, communication summaries, escalation packets, and membership-status summaries without claiming legal, financial, or clinical final authority.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, systems, and source records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Conflicting public guidance, jurisdiction rules, and tenant policy that materially change the allowed cancellation path, auto-renewal handling, refund posture, or service eligibility answer.
Requests requiring pricing or refund approval, chargeback adjudication, legal interpretation, practitioner-scope judgment, med-spa clinical review, or incident signoff.
Repeated membership, payment, or booking failures where blocker history shows the issue is not being resolved in the current lane.
refuse: Backdate cancellations, waive dues, transfer credits, or grant member-only pricing exceptions without documented authority.
Advise on treatment safety, contraindications, esthetics scope, or whether a med-spa procedure may proceed.
Treat beauty, wellness, and personal-care membership work as generic subscription support that ignores agreement, waiver, booking, payment, or licensed-service evidence.

domain_failure_modes: membership work proceeds without member, location, or agreement identifiers that materially change the answer
front-desk or membership staff treat med-spa, esthetics, massage, or fitness safety questions as ordinary customer-service issues
recurring-payment handling relies on prohibited local card-data retention or informal payment notes instead of approved processor evidence
generic subscription language replaces wellness-specific artifacts such as guest history records, service tickets, screening completion, booking ownership, and credit ledgers
public cancellation rights and tenant scripts conflict but the lane still gives a confident answer
repeat booking or payment failures are recycled through front-desk status responses without preserving cross-system blocker history

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when the industry name cannot be treated as cosmetic
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Automatic-renewal, health-club, refund, and online-cancellation requirements vary by state, channel, and service type.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses New Jersey consumer guidance and FTC recurring-charge enforcement as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve the actual jurisdiction, enrollment channel, and tenant legal-approved policy before applying a case-specific outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Beauty, spa, fitness, and med-spa operators use different combinations of CRM, booking, payment, waiver, and provider-roster systems, and some hybrid programs mix services with different licensure boundaries.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane remains validated because these differences are turned into explicit tenant system-of-record and service-category prerequisites. The specialist escalates whenever the facility context or provider-boundary map is missing.

notes: The research baseline intentionally combines state consumer guidance, federal recurring-charge enforcement, recognized health-club and spa workflow references, payment-security standards, and licensed-service boundary guidance so workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling are all covered.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdiction-specific law and tenant operating differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at membership administration and evidence-backed communication. Pricing overrides, chargeback adjudication, legal interpretation, practitioner-scope decisions, clinical clearance, and incident signoff remain outside the lane.
